AL.summary: No statewide STR ban. Local HOA and city rules may apply — verify county ordinances.

AR.summary: State law limits local ability to ban STRs in many cases. City-specific rules still vary.

AZ.summary: Generally investor-friendly statewide. Sedona and some resort towns add local licensing.

CA.summary: Statewide TOT rules apply. Cities set permits, caps, and enforcement — rules vary sharply by municipality.

CO.summary: Denver and mountain towns require licenses. Non-owner-occupied STR generally allowed with permits.

FL.summary: State preemption limits local bans. County resort taxes and registration still apply in tourist markets.

GA.summary: No statewide restrictions. Atlanta and coastal cities may require business licenses or taxes.

HI.summary: TAT license and county zoning rules required. Enforcement has increased on illegal STR operations.

ID.summary: Investor-friendly statewide. Boise and resort areas may add local registration.

IL.summary: Chicago and other cities regulate shared housing and licensing. Suburban rules vary.

IN.summary: Generally permissive. Check local zoning in college towns and lake communities.

KY.summary: No statewide STR restrictions. Louisville and Lexington may require local permits.

MD.summary: Baltimore and coastal counties require licensing. Non-owner STR often allowed with permits.

MI.summary: Investor-friendly statewide. Detroit and lake towns may have local registration.

MN.summary: Minneapolis and St. Paul require licenses. Twin Cities suburbs vary.

MO.summary: Generally permissive. Branson and KC/St. Louis may require local business licenses.

NC.summary: Investor-friendly statewide. Asheville, Charlotte, and coastal towns add local rules.

NM.summary: Santa Fe and Albuquerque require permits. Rural areas generally permissive.

NV.summary: Las Vegas and Reno require business licenses but allow non-owner STR.

NY.summary: Statewide registration and local rules vary. NYC is highly restrictive for investors.

OH.summary: Generally permissive. Columbus, Cleveland, and Cincinnati may require local registration.

OR.summary: Portland has strict rules. Other cities generally allow STR with registration.

PA.summary: Philadelphia and Pittsburgh require licenses. Suburban rules vary by township.

SC.summary: Coastal and tourist markets are STR-friendly. Some cities require business licenses.

TN.summary: State law limits local bans. Nashville and Memphis add permit and tax requirements.

TX.summary: No statewide ban. Austin, San Antonio, and DFW cities may require Type-1/Type-2 permits.

UT.summary: Generally permissive. Park City and SLC have local licensing requirements.

VA.summary: State registration required. Local zoning and permit rules vary by city and county.

WA.summary: Seattle and King County require licensing. Non-owner STR generally allowed with permits.

WI.summary: Milwaukee and Madison require permits. Rural lake markets generally permissive.

countyOverrides.summary: Unincorporated county areas require transient occupancy registration. City-specific rules apply within incorporated cities.
County vacation rental regulations apply in unincorporated areas. Gatlinburg and Pigeon Forge have separate city permits.
Unincorporated Chatham County has simpler STR rules than City of Savannah. Savannah city STVR overlay rules do not apply county-wide.
County vacation rental registration and resort tax required across municipalities.
County vacation rental certificate required in unincorporated areas. Kissimmee has separate city registration.
County accommodation tax and licensing. City of Myrtle Beach has additional requirements.

cbsaOverrides.summary: Smoky Mountain STR market — city and county permits common. Gatlinburg/Pigeon Forge/Sevierville each have rules.

cityOverrides.summary: Local Law 18 registration required. Investor STR heavily restricted in many building classes — verify registration and zoning.
Home Sharing Registration required. Non-primary units often capped at 120 hosted nights per year.
Shared housing registration required for STR. Investor operations allowed with registration and taxes.
Hotel occupancy tax and registration required. Non-owner STR generally permitted with compliance.
No city STR ban. Hotel occupancy tax applies. Verify HOA and deed restrictions.
Generally investor-friendly. Transaction privilege/sales tax and licensing may apply.
Vacation rental license required. Non-owner STR allowed with license and hotel tax.
Short-term rental registration required. Type rules vary — verify non-owner eligibility.
Transient occupancy registration required. Whole-home STR allowed with TOT compliance.
Type-1 and Type-2 STR permits required. Non-owner (Type-2) allowed with fees and inspections.
Florida preemption limits local bans. Tourist development tax and business tax receipt may apply.
No dedicated STR ban. Verify zoning and collect applicable lodging taxes.
No city-wide STR ban. Mecklenburg County occupancy tax applies. Verify HOA rules.
Generally permissive for investor STR. Innkeeper's tax and registration may apply.
Primary-residence requirement and 90-night cap for unhosted stays. Non-owner investor STR effectively prohibited.
Business and regulatory licenses required. Non-owner STR permitted with licensing.
Short-term rental license required. Active enforcement on unlicensed listings.
Short-term rental permit and hotel-motel tax required. Verify zoning district.
Generally permissive. Verify zoning and Oklahoma City business licensing.
Permit required; whole-home STR limited to primary residence in most cases.
Business license and short-term rental registration required. Non-owner STR allowed.
Short-term rental permit required within city limits.
Generally permissive. Occupancy tax and registration requirements apply.
Short-term rental license required. Non-owner STR allowed with licensing.
Tourist rooming house license required for STR operations.
Short-term rental registration required.
Short-term rental registration required within city limits.
No city-wide ban. Wake County occupancy tax applies. Verify HOA.
Resort tax and county/city registration required. Higher compliance burden than most FL markets.
Florida preemption limits bans. Hillsborough County tourist development tax applies.
Short-term rental registration required. Active enforcement in residential zones.
Short-term rental license required. Non-owner STR allowed with license and taxes.
Short-term rental license required. Non-owner STR allowed with licensing.
No dedicated STR ban. Verify zoning and collect applicable taxes.
Generally permissive. Business licensing and taxes may apply.
Short-term rental license required.
Generally permissive. Verify zoning and tax obligations.
No city-wide STR ban. Business registration and taxes may apply.
Short-term rental permit required. TOT remittance required.
Short-term rental license required.
Short-term rental registration required. Active enforcement.
Generally permissive. Verify Ada County and HOA rules.
Short-term rental registration required. Virginia statewide registry also applies.
No city-wide STR ban. Verify zoning and Jefferson County rules.
Short-term rental permit required within city limits.
STVR certificate required within overlay districts only. Non-owner 20% per-ward cap is full — new investor permits on waitlist.
Short-term rental license required. Strict enforcement in historic districts.
Generally permissive. Business license and accommodation tax may apply.
City STR permit required. Non-owner STR allowed with permit in eligible zones.
Generally permissive. Verify Madison County zoning.
Short-term vacation rental permit required.
No dedicated STR ban. Verify zoning and tax obligations.
Short-term rental license required.
Generally permissive. Verify LFUCG zoning.
Generally investor-friendly. Verify zoning and tax obligations.
Short-term rental registration required.
Short-term rental registration required.
TAT license, GET, and county registration required. Active enforcement.
Arkansas limits local bans. Generally permissive for investor STR.
Short-term rental permit required in city limits.
Florida preemption limits bans. Lee County tourist tax applies. Verify HOA.
Short-term rental registration required. Okaloosa County rules also apply.
Gatlinburg STR permit required. Sevier County also regulates vacation rentals.
Business license and accommodation tax required. Horry County rules vary by jurisdiction.
Short-term rental license required. Baldwin County rules apply in unincorporated areas.
Short-term rental permit required. Sevier County vacation rental rules also apply.
Short-term rental registration required. Tourist market with active licensing.
Vacation rental registration required. Osceola County has additional rules for unincorporated areas.
Short-term rental registration required. Active enforcement in residential zones.
Vacation rental permit required. Verify residential vs commercial zoning.
Unincorporated San Bernardino County TOT registration required. County enforcement increasing.

cityOverrides.jurisdiction: Big Bear Lake / San Bernardino County
cityOverrides.summary: City TOT and county registration required. Verify mountain zoning.

sources.label: Big Bear Lake — Transient Occupancy Tax

cityOverrides.summary: Generally permissive within Phoenix metro. Transaction privilege tax applies.
